evals.prompt: Prioritize this task: 'File quarterly BAS'. It has a deadline in 4 days. Currently no priority set. The task is in the Finance area.
evals.expected_output: Should propose P1 with a specific due date within the next 1-2 days, grounding the decision in consequence (late filing = penalties)

assertions.name: proposes_p1
assertions.expected: P1
assertions.description: Should propose P1 for a task with a deadline in 4 days

assertions.name: proposes_due_date
assertions.expected: Proposes a specific day (e.g. Monday, Tuesday, or a specific date) as the due date
assertions.description: P1 tasks always get a specific due date

assertions.name: consequence_reasoning
assertions.expected: Mentions the consequence of not doing it — late fees, penalties, or missing a legal deadline
assertions.description: Decision should be grounded in what breaks if this slips

assertions.name: no_extended_questioning
assertions.expected: Makes a direct proposal without asking clarifying questions
assertions.description: Priority is obvious here — no need to ask

evals.prompt: Prioritize this task: 'Align budget to current lifestyle'. Description: 'Review actual spending from the last few months. Create or update a budget that reflects current income, expenses, and savings goals.' No deadline. Currently no priority. It's in a Finance area.
evals.expected_output: Should propose P2 with a due date suggestion this week or next, reasoning that financial decisions have downstream consequences but no time pressure

assertions.name: proposes_p2
assertions.expected: P2
assertions.description: Important financial task with no deadline should be P2

assertions.name: proposes_due_date
assertions.expected: Suggests a specific day to work on this task
assertions.description: P2 tasks usually get a due date

assertions.name: not_p1
assertions.expected: Does not propose P1
assertions.description: No deadline, no one waiting — not urgent enough for P1

assertions.name: consequence_reasoning
assertions.expected: Mentions that this affects financial decisions or downstream planning, but nothing breaks immediately
assertions.description: Should ground the decision in consequence — important but not urgent

evals.prompt: Prioritize this task: 'Reply to energy company about meter reading'. No deadline. No one actively waiting. It's in Personal.
evals.expected_output: Should propose P3 with no due date — routine admin, do when in the right context

assertions.name: proposes_p3
assertions.expected: Proposes P3
assertions.description: Routine admin task with no urgency should be P3

assertions.name: no_due_date
assertions.expected: Does not propose or suggest setting a due date
assertions.description: P3 tasks rarely get due dates

assertions.name: keeps_it_brief
assertions.expected: Response is short — a quick proposal, not a lengthy analysis
assertions.description: Obvious priority should be fast

evals.prompt: Prioritize this task: 'Clean up iCloud folder'. No description. No deadline. No one waiting. It's in Personal.
evals.expected_output: Should propose P4 with no due date — low-stakes cleanup, backlog material

assertions.name: proposes_p4
assertions.expected: P4
assertions.description: Low-stakes cleanup task should be P4

assertions.name: no_due_date
assertions.expected: Does not propose or suggest setting a due date
assertions.description: P4 tasks never get due dates

assertions.name: keeps_it_brief
assertions.expected: Response is short — a quick proposal, not a lengthy analysis
assertions.description: Obvious low-priority tasks should be fast

evals.prompt: Re-evaluate the priority for this task: 'Research what types of insurance are required for consultants in Australia'. Currently P3, no due date. Context: the user is signing their first consulting client next week and needs insurance before starting work.
evals.expected_output: Should challenge the P3, propose bumping to P1 or P2 with a specific due date, citing the changed context

assertions.name: challenges_current_priority
assertions.expected: Acknowledges the task is currently P3 and explicitly states it should change
assertions.description: Re-evaluation should challenge the existing priority

assertions.name: proposes_bump
assertions.expected: Proposes changing priority from P3 to P1 or P2
assertions.description: Changed context (signing client next week) makes this urgent

assertions.name: proposes_due_date
assertions.expected: Proposes a specific day as due date
assertions.description: Bumped to P1/P2 means it needs a date

assertions.name: consequence_reasoning
assertions.expected: Mentions the consequence — can't start work without insurance, or risk of working uninsured
assertions.description: Bump should be grounded in what breaks if this slips

evals.prompt: Re-evaluate the priority for this task: 'Message Brad Lorge to arrange a catchup'. Currently P4, no due date. No new context — just a social catchup with a friend.
evals.expected_output: Should confirm P4 is correct, no date, move on quickly

assertions.name: confirms_p4
assertions.expected: Confirms P4 is the right priority or says it looks fine as-is
assertions.description: No reason to change priority on a social catchup

assertions.name: no_due_date
assertions.expected: Does not propose setting a due date
assertions.description: P4 tasks never get dates

assertions.name: moves_on_quickly
assertions.expected: Response is brief — confirms and moves on, no unnecessary analysis
assertions.description: When priority is obviously fine, don't dwell on it

evals.prompt: Prioritize this task: 'Organize wardrobe and donate old clothes'. No deadline. No one waiting. It's in Personal.
evals.expected_output: Should default to P3 or P4 — nothing breaks if this slips. Should not inflate to P2.

assertions.name: defaults_low
assertions.expected: Proposes P3 or P4, not P1 or P2
assertions.description: Should default low — nothing breaks if this slips a week

assertions.name: no_due_date
assertions.expected: Does not propose a due date
assertions.description: Low-priority tasks should not be dated

assertions.name: no_priority_inflation
assertions.expected: Does not treat this as important or urgent
assertions.description: Protects P1/P2 from inflation — tidying tasks are low-stakes

evals.prompt: Prioritize this task: 'Submit grant application'. Description: 'The grant portal closes on April 2nd.' No current priority. It's in a Work area. Today is March 25.
evals.expected_output: Should propose P1 with a due date before April 2, and correctly distinguish the April 2 deadline from the due date

assertions.name: proposes_p1
assertions.expected: P1
assertions.description: Hard external deadline 8 days away should be P1

assertions.name: proposes_due_date
assertions.expected: Proposes a specific working day before April 2 as the due date
assertions.description: P1 tasks always get a due date — and it should be before the deadline

assertions.name: deadline_vs_due_date
assertions.expected: Treats April 2 as the hard deadline and suggests a separate due date for when to work on it, or at minimum acknowledges the portal close date as a fixed constraint distinct from the scheduling date
assertions.description: Should distinguish between the hard deadline (portal closes) and the scheduling intention (when to do the work)

evals.prompt: Prioritize this task: 'Look into switching energy providers'. No description. No deadline. No one waiting. It's in Personal.
evals.expected_output: Should ask what happens if this slips, since importance is ambiguous — could be P2 if bills are high, P3 if just idle curiosity

assertions.name: asks_consequence_question
assertions.expected: Asks about consequences of not doing this soon, or what happens if it slips
assertions.description: Ambiguous importance — should ask the one clarifying question before proposing

assertions.name: one_question_max
assertions.expected: Asks at most one clarifying question, not multiple
assertions.description: One question max — this is a quick decision

evals.prompt: Re-evaluate the priority for this task: 'Write blog post about project management tips'. Currently P2 with a due date of last Monday. It was not completed. No external deadline. No one is waiting on it.
evals.expected_output: Should challenge the P2 — it missed its date with no consequences, suggesting it might actually be P3. Should propose dropping priority and removing the due date.

assertions.name: challenges_p2
assertions.expected: Questions whether P2 is still the right priority, given it was missed without consequence
assertions.description: A missed P2 with no fallout suggests priority was inflated

assertions.name: proposes_downgrade
assertions.expected: Suggests dropping to P3 or P4
assertions.description: If missing it caused no problems, it probably isn't P2

assertions.name: addresses_due_date
assertions.expected: Suggests removing the due date or not re-dating it if dropped to P3/P4
assertions.description: P3/P4 tasks should not have due dates

evals.prompt: Prioritize this task: 'Send API credentials to contractor'. Description: 'They can't start their work until they have these.' No deadline. Currently no priority. It's in a Work area.
evals.expected_output: Should propose P1 or P2 with a due date — someone is actively blocked waiting on this output

assertions.name: proposes_p1_or_p2
assertions.expected: Proposes P1 or P2
assertions.description: Blocking someone else should be at least P2

assertions.name: proposes_due_date
assertions.expected: Proposes a specific day as the due date
assertions.description: P1/P2 tasks get due dates

assertions.name: blocking_reasoning
assertions.expected: Mentions that someone is waiting or blocked, grounding urgency in the fact that another person can't proceed
assertions.description: Time-sensitivity should come from blocking others, not an arbitrary deadline

evals.prompt: Re-evaluate the priority for this task: 'Review and update client onboarding checklist'. Currently P1 with no due date. No external deadline. No one is actively waiting on it.
evals.expected_output: Should flag that a P1 task without a due date is inconsistent — either add a date or reconsider the priority

assertions.name: flags_missing_date
assertions.expected: Points out that a P1 task should have a due date and either proposes one or questions whether P1 is correct
assertions.description: P1 tasks always get a due date — missing one is a signal something is off

assertions.name: challenges_or_fixes
assertions.expected: Either proposes adding a specific due date to keep it P1, or suggests downgrading the priority since there's no deadline or blocking dependency
assertions.description: Should resolve the inconsistency one way or the other

evals.prompt: Re-evaluate the priority for this task: 'Sort through old receipts'. Currently P3 with a due date of next Wednesday. No deadline. No one waiting. It's in Personal.
evals.expected_output: Should flag the inconsistency — P3 tasks rarely have due dates. Either bump to P2 or remove the date.

assertions.name: flags_date_inconsistency
assertions.expected: Points out that a P3 task having a due date is unusual or inconsistent
assertions.description: P3 tasks rarely get due dates — this mismatch should be called out

assertions.name: proposes_resolution
assertions.expected: Suggests either removing the due date to keep it P3, or bumping priority to P2 if the date is justified
assertions.description: Should resolve the priority/date mismatch in one direction

evals.prompt: Prioritize this task: 'Book flights for conference'. Description: 'Conference is June 15. Prices are going up — cheapest fares disappear in the next few days.' No current priority. It's in Work.
evals.expected_output: Should propose P1 or P2 based on the closing window — opportunity disappears if you wait

assertions.name: proposes_high_priority
assertions.expected: Proposes P1 or P2
assertions.description: Closing window (fares disappearing) makes this time-sensitive

assertions.name: closing_window_reasoning
assertions.expected: Mentions the closing window — prices rising, cheap fares disappearing, or the opportunity cost of waiting
assertions.description: Time-sensitivity should be grounded in the closing window, not just the conference date

assertions.name: proposes_due_date
assertions.expected: Proposes a specific day as due date, within the next few days
assertions.description: P1/P2 tasks get due dates, and the window is closing soon
